user.title: Notification Center
user.description: Create the Notification Center panel. These defaults apply to every notification unless an individual notification overrides them.

data.name: Notification Center name
data.persistence: Default time to keep alerts active

data_description.name: Name shown for this integration in Home Assistant.
data_description.persistence: How long an alert remains active after it is triggered, unless that notification clears sooner.
data_description.persistence_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for the default active time.
data_description.mute_duration: How long a user-triggered mute hides an active alert before it appears again.
data_description.mute_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for the default mute time.
data_description.devices: Mobile app devices used when a notification does not choose its own delivery targets. Leave empty for no default delivery.

error.single_instance_allowed: Only one Notification Center instance is supported.
error.test_failed: The notification test could not be sent. Check the Home Assistant log for details.

settings.title: Panel settings
settings.description: Set the default active and mute times used by notifications that do not define their own overrides.

data.persistence: Default time to keep alerts active

data_description.persistence: How long an alert remains active after it is triggered, unless its evaluation clears sooner.
data_description.persistence_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for the default active time.
data_description.mute_duration: How long an active alert stays hidden when a dashboard user mutes it.
data_description.mute_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for the default mute time.

add_notification.title: Add notification
add_notification.description: Define what Home Assistant should evaluate, what each result means, and which mobile devices should receive the alert.

data.name: Display name
data.mode: How should this notification be evaluated?
data.entity: Entity for a simple on/off check
data.outcomes: Messages and appearance for each result
data.persistence: Time to keep this alert active (optional)

data_description.name: Friendly name shown on dashboard cards and in mobile notifications.
data_description.id: Stable internal key used by cards and actions. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores; do not change it after automations depend on it.
data_description.mode: Choose Boolean for an on/off result. Choose Multiple outcomes when a template can return several named values.
data_description.entity: For a simple Boolean notification, select the entity whose on/off state should be checked. Leave empty when the Jinja template supplies the result.
data_description.template: Optional Jinja that returns true/false, on/off, or one outcome key. Use this when the result depends on multiple entities, attributes, or calculations.
data_description.outcomes: Map each possible returned value to active, title, message, icon, severity, and optional css fields. The editor accepts YAML or JSON objects and can expand to full screen.
data_description.devices: Choose one or more Home Assistant mobile app devices. Leave empty to use no delivery targets for this notification.
data_description.persistence: Optional override for how long this alert stays active. Leave blank to use Panel settings.
data_description.persistence_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for this notification's active time override.
data_description.mute_duration: Optional override for how long this alert stays hidden after a user mutes it. Leave blank to use Panel settings.
data_description.mute_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for this notification's mute override.

edit_notification.title: Edit notification
edit_notification.description: Choose a notification, then review its evaluation, result messages, mobile devices, and timing overrides.

data.name: Display name
data.mode: How should this notification be evaluated?
data.entity: Entity for a simple on/off check
data.outcomes: Messages and appearance for each result
data.persistence: Time to keep this alert active (optional)

data_description.selected_id: Choose the notification you want to update.
data_description.name: Friendly name shown on dashboard cards and in mobile notifications.
data_description.id: Stable internal key used by cards and actions. Existing IDs remain unchanged when editing.
data_description.mode: Choose Boolean for an on/off result. Choose Multiple outcomes when a template can return several named values.
data_description.entity: For a simple Boolean notification, select the entity whose on/off state should be checked. Leave empty when the Jinja template supplies the result.
data_description.template: Optional Jinja that returns true/false, on/off, or one outcome key. Use this when the result depends on multiple entities, attributes, or calculations.
data_description.outcomes: Map each possible returned value to active, title, message, icon, severity, and optional css fields. The editor accepts YAML or JSON objects and can expand to full screen.
data_description.devices: Choose one or more Home Assistant mobile app devices. Leave empty to use no delivery targets for this notification.
data_description.persistence: Optional override for how long this alert stays active. Leave blank to use Panel settings.
data_description.persistence_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for this notification's active time override.
data_description.mute_duration: Optional override for how long this alert stays hidden after a user mutes it. Leave blank to use Panel settings.
data_description.mute_unit: Minutes, hours, or days for this notification's mute override.

test_notification.title: Test notification delivery
test_notification.description: Send a test message to the selected notification's configured mobile devices without changing its active or muted state.

data_description.selected_id: Choose the notification whose configured message and target devices you want to test.

test_outcome.title: Choose test outcome
test_outcome.description: The test sends the selected outcome's title and message. Current template result evaluates the notification exactly as it is now.

remove_notification.title: Remove notification
remove_notification.description: Permanently remove a notification definition from Notification Center. This does not delete its source entities or devices.

data_description.selected_id: Choose the definition to remove. Any dashboard card set to All will stop showing it automatically.
